# ENT & Allergy — Dr. Christopher Kennel FRACS
> Advanced ear, nose, throat, and head & neck surgery in Hastings, Hawke's Bay, New Zealand.

## About
Dr. Christopher (Chris) Kennel is a fellowship-trained otolaryngologist (ENT surgeon) based at the Kaweka Specialist Centre, Hastings, Hawke's Bay, New Zealand. He is a Fellow of the Royal Australasian College of Surgeons (FRACS) and a Diplomate of the American Board of Otolaryngology — Head & Neck Surgery. He completed an advanced fellowship in otology and neurotology in Delaware, USA, and a fellowship with the American Academy of Otolaryngic Allergy. He moved from the United States to Hawke's Bay in 2022.

He is the only FRACS (ORL) surgeon based in Hawke's Bay. He regularly sees patients from Gisborne and Tairāwhiti, where there is no local ENT surgeon. Patients from anywhere in New Zealand are welcome.

## Practice
- **Name:** ENT & Allergy
- **Website:** https://entallergy.nz/
- **Phone:** 06 651 4070
- **Address:** Level 2, Kaweka Specialist Centre, 209 Canning Road, Hastings, Hawke's Bay 4120, New Zealand
- **Hours:** Monday–Friday 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM
- **Self-referrals:** Welcome — no GP referral required

## Credentials
- Fellow of the Royal Australasian College of Surgeons (FRACS)
- Diplomate of the American Board of Otolaryngology — Head & Neck Surgery
- Advanced fellowship in otology and neurotology (Delaware, USA)
- Fellowship with the American Academy of Otolaryngic Allergy
- Vocational registration as a specialist with the Medical Council of New Zealand
- Contract surgeon for Health New Zealand

## Surgical Specialties
- Otology and neurotology: stapedectomy, stapedotomy, mastoidectomy, tympanoplasty, myringoplasty, cholesteatoma surgery, canalplasty (surfer's ear / exostoses), Osia implant / bone anchored hearing device, tympanic membrane perforation repair, ossiculoplasty, pre-auricular cyst excision
- Rhinology and sinus surgery: functional endoscopic sinus surgery (FESS) including frontal sinus drill out or Carolyn's window, septoplasty, turbinate reduction, nasal polyp surgery, image-guided navigation surgery
- Head and neck surgery: neck dissection, parotid surgery (facelift approach), transoral Zenker's diverticulum surgery, salivary gland surgery, cricopharyngeal myotomy, excisional biopsies, lymph node excisions, branchial cleft cyst excisions
- Head and neck oncology: cancer surgery, clear margin rates published annually
- Skin cancer surgery: Basal cell carcinoma (BCC), squamous cell carcinoma (SCC), melanoma, benign lesions, facial reconstruction with flaps and grafts, blepharoplasty
- Throat surgery: coblation tonsillectomy, adenotonsillectomy, Botox for retrograde cricopharyngeal dysfunction (R-CPD)
- Hearing devices: Osia surgically implanted hearing device (one of the first surgeons in New Zealand to implant this device)
- In-clinic procedures: nasal endoscopy, flexible laryngoscopy, nasal cautery, ear microsuction, ultrasound-guided fine needle aspiration (FNA) biopsy, nerve blocks, Botox for dystonia or sialorrhea, skin lesion and cancer excisions, ear ventilation tubes (grommets), 

## Audited Surgical Outcomes (November 2025)
- Coblation tonsillectomy readmission (bleeding, pain, dehydration): 0%
- Mastoidectomy facial nerve injury: 0%
- Parotidectomy permanent facial nerve injury: 0%
- Head & neck cancer clear surgical margins: 92.9%
- Melanoma clear surgical margins: 100%
- Non-melanoma skin cancer (BCC & SCC) clear surgical margins: 94.7%
- Non-skin surgical site infection: 1.6%
- 30-day postoperative mortality: 0%

## Key Differentiators
- No GP referral required — self-referrals welcome
- 24/7 direct mobile access to Dr. Kennel for all surgical patients
- Published annual surgical outcome audit — one of the few ENT surgeons in New Zealand to do this
- Coblation tonsillectomy: first surgeon in the Hawke's Bay region to offer this technique
- Osia implant: one of the first surgeons in New Zealand to implant this device
- Parotid surgery via facelift (minimal incision) approach with fat grafting — unique in Hawke's Bay
- Transoral Zenker's diverticulum surgery — unique equipment in the region
- Image-guided sinus surgery using patient's own CT/MRI
- Contract surgeon for Health New Zealand — treats both public and private patients
